details: On `npm install`, the package's preinstall hook executes index.js, which collects host reconnaissance data (os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, __dirname, process.cwd()) and exfiltrates it via two channels: (1) a hex-encoded DNS A-record lookup to a subdomain of *.oast.live (interactsh-style out-of-band canary), and (2) an HTTP POST of the same JSON payload to the hardcoded IP 172.201.213.59 on port 9090 path /c. The package has no advertised functionality — package.json describes it only as 'security research' and ships no implementation beyond the beacon. The scope name (@klapp-login-platform) and inflated version (99.0.0) match the textbook dependency-confusion shape, designed to win resolution over a private internal package of the same name and silently fingerprint corporate build environments.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
